Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can effectively optimize mixed-precision quantization for deep neural networks, leading to improved accuracy and efficiency.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization, but they rely on heuristic-based methods or uniform precision allocation. Our approach differs by incorporating curvature-awareness and deterministic numerical guardrails to optimize precision routing.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for mixed-precision quantization in deep neural networks. By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, our approach optimizes precision allocation to minimize accuracy loss while meeting efficiency targets. We evaluate our framework on various benchmark models and demonstrate significant improvements in accuracy and efficiency compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on ImageNet with ResNet and MobileNet families
Compare the performance of our approach with existing mixed-precision quantization methods (e.g., EMQ, SNIP, AMP-ViT)
Investigate the impact of curvature-awareness on precision routing and accuracy
Assess the efficiency of our framework in terms of computational overhead and memory usage
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require additional computational overhead for curvature estimation and optimization
The approach may not be suitable for very large models or datasets due to memory and computational constraints
The effectiveness of the framework may depend on the specific deep neural network architecture and task
